text: my own therapist to talk me out of beating the shit out of them. Trista says she’s scared she could be the same to our baby as her parents were to her, but anyone with eyes could see that if she loves our kid half as much as she loves all those critters down below us, this baby will live a full and happy life. And I’ll be there to help her through that. I pull Trista closer to me and press my lips to hers. “I love when you say ‘our baby.’” She smiles against my smile. “Well, she is ours and as much as I love you, I just want you to know you don’t need to comfort me through all my therapy stuff. I can handle it.” I glower down at her. “I will comfort my girlfriend anytime I damn well please.” She shakes with laughter, losing it every time I call her my girlfriend. I will admit it feels juvenile, especially with all we’ve gone through together, but she won’t let me put a ring on her finger yet, so it is what it is. “Fine, you can comfort me. Just know that it’ll take some time for me to get used to you doing stuff for me because I didn’t have that growing up. But I am working on it, I promise.” My eyes move from her lips to her eyes and back down to her lips. “You’re amazing.” “I know I am,” she replies, and I feel her inhale deeply as I kiss her forehead. “This baby will be even more amazing than me! Can you imagine the thought?” “She will be a force of nature just like her mama.” I move around Trista to hold her from behind and take in the barn apartment. This place has been through some crazy times, but nothing ever as significant as this moment right here. “And you’re sure we’re not moving things along too fast, right? Me moving in with you I mean?” Trista tips her head back to look up at me. “I really want this to work between us.” “We work.” I squeeze her tight to feel the certainly in my bones. “And it was either you move into my place, or I move into yours because I don’t like being away from you two at night.” I move my hands over her belly possessively. “And not for nothing, but my place smells better.” “I like the smell in here!” Trista argues proudly. “You would,” I murmur, and press my lips to her neck, inhaling her rosy scent. “And you’ll still get plenty of time up here when we turn this space into your office.” “My own office,” she says the words like they’re still a dream, a far off fantasy that she can’t quite visualize yet. But she has no idea what lengths I’ll go through to make her dreams a reality. Because this lucky woman right here has already made all my dreams come true. Chapter 46 Weeks Pregnant: 41 Animals on the Mountain: 12 Trista “Trista, I’m not fucking around here anymore,” Wyatt growls angrily under his breath, pointing to the barn door. “I’m starting the truck, and you will join me in five minutes or so help me God, I will carry you off this mountain.” I laugh and rub my swollen belly that’s sticking out of my Carhartt coat that I can no longer zip. “It’ll take an act of God to lift me.” “Try me.” The muscle in his jaw pops angrily as he gazes down at me with heat in his eyes. If I didn’t know any better, I’d say he likes fighting with me. Perhaps it brings back memories of him bossing me around in the early days of this pregnancy. A lot has changed since then. I can no longer see my bajingo, for one. And I’m pretty sure I’m wearing two different shoes. Wyatt stops at Millie’s pen to give her one last pat on the head before he continues on into the snowy tundra. It’s Christmas Eve and he’s been riding my ass about getting off the mountain ever since my due date has come and gone. He made plans for us to stay at Max’s house until the baby arrives because he doesn’t want to get snowed in and have me go into labor on the mountain. But I’ve been dragging my feet because I hate the idea of leaving all the animals. We are nearly at capacity on Mount Millie, as I’ve taken to calling it. Millie greets every new furry friend that comes up here, so it seems only right this peak should be named after our hostess. She loves all her new friends just as much as I do. The feeling of contentment I get seeing every stall filled with a happy animal has me fixing to burst—and not just with child. With sublime happiness. This winter has felt like I’m living in a Hallmark movie up here. It’s no wonder it’s been so hard to leave. But the more snow that comes, the grumpier my mountain man gets, so I suppose it’s time to give in. “Promise me, you’ll look at my instructions every day.” I point to the big chalkboard mounted on the wall as I heavily exhale. Just the simple act of walking is harder these days. Fighting to catch my breath, I add, “Check off each one daily so you don’t forget anyone.” “We got it, Mama Bear,” Calder drones as he feeds a bit of hay to Trumpet, the alpaca. “We’ll text you if we have questions,” Luke confirms. “We can handle this.” I turn to Reginald’s pen last, and my stomach tightens. “And don’t forget, Reggie doesn’t like to wake up early, but you still need to feed him early. If he wakes up and his trough is empty, he will be a pill for you the whole day. I’m not exaggerating. Try
